frameworks.name: rational analysis of memory
frameworks.one_line: memory optimized to store information needed in the future under precision constraints

frameworks.name: rate-distortion theory
frameworks.one_line: formalizes trade-off between memory precision and task performance

core_question: Can regularities of memory be explained as the solution to a rational optimization problem balancing precision against future utility?
key_assumptions: memory is optimized for future information demands
information can only be stored with limited precision
memory regularities reflect rational design principles
key_findings: diverse memory regularities can be derived from rate-distortion design principles
cognitive and environmental factors shape these regularities in predictable ways
interpretations: memory's design follows rational principles of constrained optimization for anticipated future use
